            return str(self.expected[0])\n94\t            elif len(self.expected) == 2:\n95\t                return f\"{self.expected[0]} or {self.expected[1]}\"\n96\t            else:\n97\t content-type(s)\n344\t    - The response URL (if available)\n345\t    \"\"\"\n346\t\n347\t    def __init__(self,\n348\t                 actual: str,\n349\t                 expected: Union[str, List[str]],\n350\t                 url: Optional[str] = None):\n351\t        \"\"\"\n352\t        Initialize a content-type validation error.\n353\t\n354\t        Args:\n355\t            actual: The actual content-type header value\n356\t            expected: The expected content-type or list of allowed types\n357\t            url: Optional URL that was requested\n358\t        \"\"\"\n359\t        self.actual = actual\n360\t        self.expected = expected\n361\t        self.url = url\n362\t\n363\t        # Build detailed error message\n364\t        msg_parts = []\n365\t\n366\t        if url:\n367\t            msg_parts.append(f\"URL: {url}\")\n368\t\n369\t        msg_parts.append(f\"Expected Content-Type: {self._format_expected()}\")\n370\t        msg_parts.append(f\"Actual Content-Type: {actual}\")\n371\t\n372\t        message = \"\\n  \".join(msg_parts)\n373\t        super().__init__(message)\n374\t\n375\t    def _format_expected(self) -> str:\n376\t        \"\"\"Format expected content-types for display.\"\"\"\n377\t        if isinstance(self.expected, list):\n378\t            if len(self.expected) == 1:\n379\t                return str(self.expected[0])\n380\t            elif len(self.expected) == 2:\n381\t                return f\"{self.expected[0]} or {self.expected[1]}\"\n382\t            else:\n383\t                return f\"one of {self.expected}\"\n384\t        else:\n385\t            return str(self.expected)\n386\t\n387\t\n388\tdef _normalize_content_type(content_type: Optional[str]) -> str:\n389\t    \"\"\"\n390\t    Normalize a content-type string for comparison.\n391\t\n392\t    This function:\n393\t    - Converts to lowercase\n394\t    - Trims whitespace\n395\t    - Extracts the MIME type without parameters (e.g., 'application/json; charset=utf-8' -> 'application/json')\n396\t\n397\t    Args:\n398\t        content_type: The content-type string to normalize (can be None)\n399\t\n400\t    Returns:\n401\t        str: Normalized content-type, or empty string if input is None\n402\t    \"\"\"\n403\t    if not content_type:\n404\t        return \"\"\n405\t\n406\t    # Convert to lowercase and trim whitespace\n407\t    content_type = content_type.lower().strip()\n408\t\n409\t    # Extract only the MIME type (before semicolon if present)\n410\t    # This handles cases like 'application/json; charset=utf-8' -> 'application/json'\n411\t    if ';' in content_type:\n412\t        content_type = content_type.split(';')[0].strip()\n413\t\n414\t    return content_type\n415\t\n416\t\n417\tdef validate_content_type(\n418\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n419\t    expected_type: Union[str, List[str]],\n420\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n421\t) -> bool:\n422\t    \"\"\"\n423\t    Validate HTTP response content-type header against expected value(s).\n424\t\n425\t    This helper function validates that an HTTP response has the expected content-type.\n426\t    It supports both single content-types and lists of allowed types, making it\n427\t    flexible for different testing scenarios. The function uses pattern matching,\n428\t    so 'application/json' will match 'application/json; charset=utf-8'.\n429\t\n430\t    Args:\n431\t        response: HTTP response object (requests.Response) or tuple of (status_code, headers, body)\n432\t        expected_type: Expected content-type (str) or list of allowed types (List[str])\n433\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws ContentTypeValidationError on validation failure.\n434\t                       If False, returns False instead.\n435\t\n436\t    Returns:\n437\t        bool: True if content-type matches expected value(s), False otherwise\n438\t\n439\t    Raises:\n440\t        ContentTypeValidationError: If validation fails and throw_on_error is True\n441\t        TypeError: If response or expected_type has invalid type\n442\t\n443\t    Examples:\n444\t        >>> # Single content-type validation\n445\t        >>> response = requests.get('http://example.com/api')\n446\t        >>> validate_content_type(response, 'application/json')\n447\t\n448\t        >>> # Multiple allowed content-types\n449\t        >>> validate_content_type(response, ['application/json', 'application/xml'])\n450\t\n451\t        >>> # Pattern matching (matches both 'application/json' and 'application/json; charset=utf-8')\n452\t        >>> validate_content_type(response, 'application/json')\n453\t\n454\t        >>> # Using tuple response format\n455\t        >>> status, headers, body = curl_request(...)\n456\t        >>> validate_content_type((status, headers, body), 'text/html')\n457\t\n458\t        >>> # Non-throwing validation\n459\t        >>> is_valid = validate_content_type(response, 'application/json', throw_on_error=False)\n460\t        >>> if is_valid:\n461\t        ...     print(\"Content-type is valid!\")\n462\t\n463\t    Acceptance Criteria:\n464\t    - Function accepts a response object and expected content-type(s)\n465\t    - Supports pattern matching (e.g., 'application/json' matches 'application/json; charset=utf-8')\n466\t    - Returns boolean or throws assertion error with clear message\n467\t    - Includes test cases demonstrating various content-type scenarios\n468\t    - Function is exported from test utils module\n469\t    \"\"\"\n470\t    # Extract content-type and URL from response\n471\t    actual_content_type: Optional[str] = None\n472\t    url: Optional[str] = None\n473\t\n474\t    # Check for response-like objects (duck typing)\n475\t    # First check if it's a tuple (special case)\n476\t    if isinstance(response, tuple) and len(response) >= 2:\n477\t        # Tuple format: (status_code, headers, body) or (status_code, headers)\n478\t        if len(response) >= 2:\n479\t            headers = response[1]\n480\t            if isinstance(headers, dict):\n481\t                actual_content_type = headers.get('Content-Type', headers.get('content-type'))\n482\t            elif isinstance(headers, str):\n483\t                # Headers might be a string in some test scenarios\n484\t                actual_content_type = headers\n485\t    # Then check if it has response-like attributes (headers, etc.)\n486\t    elif hasattr(response, 'headers'):\n487\t        actual_content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type',\n488\t                                                     response.headers.get('content-type', ''))\n489\t        url = getattr(response, 'url', None)\n490\t    else:\n491\t        raise TypeError(\n492\t            f\"Response must be response-like (with headers attribute) or tuple (status, headers, body), \"\n493\t            f\"got {type(response).__name__}\"\n494\t        )\n495\t\n496\t    # Normalize the actual content-type for comparison\n497\t    normalized_actual = _normalize_content_type(actual_content_type)\n498\t\n499\t    # Normalize expected_type to a list for uniform checking\n500\t    expected_types: List[str]\n501\t    if isinstance(expected_type, str):\n502\t        expected_types = [expected_type]\n503\t    elif isinstance(expected_type, list):\n504\t        expected_types = expected_type\n505\t    else:\n506\t        raise TypeError(\n507\t            f\"expected_type must be str or List[str], \"\n508\t            f\"got {type(expected_type).__name__}\"\n509\t        )\n510\t\n511\t    # Validate all expected types are strings and normalize them\n512\t    normalized_expected = []\n513\t    for ct in expected_types:\n514\t        if not isinstance(ct, str):\n515\t            raise TypeError(\n516\t                f\"All content-types must be strings, got {type(ct).__name__}\"\n517\t            )\n518\t        normalized_expected.append(_normalize_content_type(ct))\n519\t\n520\t    # Check if actual content-type matches any expected type\n521\t    is_valid = normalized_actual in normalized_expected\n522\t\n523\t    if not is_valid and throw_on_error:\n524\t        # Use the original expected_type (not normalized) for error message\n525\t        error_expected = expected_type\n526\t        raise ContentTypeValidationError(\n527\t            actual=actual_content_type or \"(missing)\",\n528\t            expected=error_expected,\n529\t            url=url\n530\t        )\n531\t\n532\t    return is_valid\n533\t\n534\t\n535\tdef validate_json_content_type(\n536\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n537\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n538\t) -> bool:\n539\t    \"\"\"\n540\t    Validate response has JSON content-type.\n541\t\n542\t    Accepts any JSON content-type variant (application/json, text/json, etc.).\n543\t\n544\t    Args:\n545\t        response: HTTP response object\n546\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws on validation failure\n547\t\n548\t    Returns:\n549\t        bool: True if content-type indicates JSON\n550\t    \"\"\"\n551\t    json_types = [\n552\t        'application/json',\n553\t        'text/json',\n554\t        'application/vnd.api+json',\n555\t        'application/problem+json'\n556\t    ]\n557\t    return validate_content_type(\n558\t        response,\n559\t        expected_type=json_types,\n560\t        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n561\t    )\n562\t\n563\t\n564\tdef validate_xml_content_type(\n565\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n566\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n567\t) -> bool:\n568\t    \"\"\"\n569\t    Validate response has XML content-type.\n570\t\n571\t    Accepts any XML content-type variant (application/xml, text/xml, etc.).\n572\t\n573\t    Args:\n574\t        response: HTTP response object\n575\t        throw_on_error:       'text/xml',\n583\t        'application/vnd+xml',\n584\t        'application/rss+xml',\n585\t        'application/atom+xml'\n586\t    ]\n587\t    return validate_content_type(\n588\t        response,\n589\t        expected_type=xml_types,\n590\t        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n591\t    )\n592\t\n593\t\n594\tdef validate_html_content_type(\n595\t    response: content-type variant (text/html, application/xhtml+xml, etc.).\n602\t\n603\t    Args:\n604\t        response: HTTP response object\n605\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws on validation failure\n606\t\n607\t    Returns:\n608\t        bool: True if content-type indicates HTML\n609\t    \"\"\"\n610\t    html_types = [\n611\t        'text/html',\n612\t        'application/xhtml+xml'\n613\t    ]\n614\t    return validate_content_type(\n615\t        response,\n616\t        expected_type=html_types,\n617\t        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n618\t    )\n619\t\n620\t\n621\tdef validate_text_content_type(\n622\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n623\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n624\t) -> bool:\n625\t    \"\"\"\n626\t    Validate response has plain text content-type.\n627\t\n628\t    Accepts text/plain content-type.\n629\t\n630\t    Args:\n631\t        response: HTTP response object\n632\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws on validation failure\n633\t\n634\t    Returns:\n635\t        bool: True if content-type indicates plain text\n636\t    \"\"\"\n637\t    return validate_content_type(\n638\t        response,\n639\t        expected_type='text/plain',\n640\t        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n641\t    )\n642\t\n643\t\n644\t# =============================================================================\n645\t# CORS HEADER VALIDATION\n646\t# =============================================================================\n647\t\n648\tclass CORSValidationError(AssertionError):\n649\t    \"\"\"\n650\t    Custom assertion error for CORS header validation failures.\n651\t\n652\t    Provides clear, formatted error messages that show:\n653\t    - The actual CORS headers received\n654\t    - The expected CORS headers or origin values\n655\t    - The response URL (if available)\n656\t    \"\"\"\n657\t\n658\t    def __init__(self,\n659\t                 message: str,\n660\t                 actual_headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,\n661\t                 expected_origin: Optional[str] = None,\n662\t                 url: Optional[str] = None):\n663\t        \"\"\"\n664\t        Initialize a CORS validation error.\n665\t\n666\t        Args:\n667\t            message: The main error message\n668\t            actual_headers: The actual CORS headers received\n669\t            expected_origin: The expected origin value\n670\t            url: Optional URL that was requested\n671\t        \"\"\"\n672\t        self.actual_headers = actual_headers or {}\n673\t        self.expected_origin = expected_origin\n674\t        self.url = url\n675\t\n676\t        # Build detailed error message\n677\t        msg_parts = []\n678\t\n679\t        if url:\n680\t            msg_parts.append(f\"URL: {url}\")\n681\t\n682\t        msg_parts.append(message)\n683\t\n684\t        if expected_origin:\n685\t            msg_parts.append(f\"Expected origin: {expected_origin}\")\n686\t\n687\t        if actual_headers:\n688\t            msg_parts.append(\"Actual CORS headers:\")\n689\t            for header, value in actual_headers.items():\n690\t                msg_parts.append(f\"  {header}: {value}\")\n691\t\n692\t        super().__init__(\"\\n  \".join(msg_parts))\n693\t\n694\t\n695\tdef _extract_cors_headers(response: Union[requests.Response, tuple]) -> Dict[str, str]:\n696\t    \"\"\"\n697\t    Extract CORS headers from a response object.\n698\t\n699\t    Args:\n700\t        response: HTTP response object (requests.Response) or tuple of (status_code, headers, body)\n701\t\n702\t    Returns:\n703\t        Dict[str, str]: Dictionary of CORS headers found in the response\n704\t    \"\"\"\n705\t    cors_headers = {}\n706\t\n707\t    # Common CORS header names (both lowercase and titlecase)\n708\t    cors_header_names = [\n709\t        'access-control-allow-origin',\n710\t        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin',\n711\t        'access-control-allow-methods',\n712\t        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods',\n713\t        'access-control-allow-headers',\n714\t        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers',\n715\t        'access-control-allow-credentials',\n716\t        'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials',\n717\t        'access-control-expose-headers',\n718\t        'Access-Control-Expose-Headers',\n719\t        'access-control-max-age',\n720\t        'Access-Control-Max-Age',\n721\t    ]\n722\t\n723\t    # Check for response-like objects (duck typing)\n724\t    if isinstance(response, tuple) and len(response) >= 2:\n725\t        # Tuple format: (status_code, headers, body) or (status_code, headers)\n726\t        headers = response[1]\n727\t        if isinstance(headers, dict):\n728\t            for header_name in cors_header_names:\n729\t                if header_name in headers:\n730\t                    # Normalize to lowercase for consistency\n731\t                    normalized_name = header_name.lower()\n732\t                    cors_headers[normalized_name] = headers[header_name]\n733\t    elif hasattr(response, 'headers'):\n734\t        # requests.Response-like object\n735\t        for header_name in cors_header_names:\n736\t            if header_name in response.headers:\n737\t                normalized_name = header_name.lower()\n738\t                cors_headers[normalized_name] = response.headers[header_name]\n739\t\n740\t    return cors_headers\n741\t\n742\t\n743\tdef validate_cors_headers(\n744\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n745\t    expected_origin: Optional[str] = None,\n746\t    allow_wildcard: bool = True,\n747\t    require_allow_origin: bool = True,\n748\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n749\t) -> bool:\n750\t    \"\"\"\n751\t    Validate CORS headers in an HTTP response.\n752\t\n753\t    This helper function validates that an HTTP response has proper CORS headers.\n754\t    It supports checking for the presence of Access-Control-Allow-Origin, validating\n755\t    specific origin values, and distinguishing between wildcard (*) and specific origins.\n756\t\n757\t    Args:\n758\t        response: HTTP response object (requests.Response) or tuple of (status_code, headers, body)\n759\t        expected_origin: Optional specific origin value to validate (e.g., 'https://example.com')\n760\t                        If None, only checks for presence of CORS headers\n761\t        allow_wildcard: If True, accepts wildcard (*) as a valid origin\n762\t        require_allow_origin: If True, requires Access-Control-Allow-Origin header to be present\n763\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws CORSValidationError on validation failure.\n764\t                       If False, returns False instead.\n765\t\n766\t    Returns:\n767\t        bool: True if CORS headers are valid, False otherwise\n768\t\n769\t    Raises:\n770\t        CORSValidationError: If validation fails and throw_on_error is True\n771\t        TypeError: If response has invalid type\n772\t\n773\t    Examples:\n774\t        >>> # Check for any CORS headers present\n775\t        >>> response = requests.get('http://example.com/api')\n776\t        >>> validate_cors_headers(response)\n777\t\n778\t        >>> # Validate specific origin\n779\t        >>> validate_cors_headers(response, expected_origin='https://myapp.com')\n780\t\n781\t        >>> # Reject wildcard, require specific origin\n782\t        >>> validate_cors_headers(response, expected_origin='https://myapp.com', allow_wildcard=False)\n783\t\n784\t        >>> # Using tuple response format\n785\t        >>> status, headers, body = curl_request(...)\n786\t        >>> validate_cors_headers((status, headers, body), expected_origin='https://example.com')\n787\t\n788\t        >>> # Non-throwing validation\n789\t        >>> is_valid = validate_cors_headers(response, expected_origin='https://example.com', throw_on_error=False)\n790\t        >>> if is_valid:\n791\t        ...     print(\"CORS headers are valid!\")\n792\t\n793\t    Acceptance Criteria:\n794\t    - Function accepts a response object and validates required CORS headers\n795\t    - Checks for Access-Control-Allow-Origin header\n796\t    - Optionally validates specific origin values\n797\t    - Supports checking for wildcard vs specific origins\n798\t    - Returns boolean or throws assertion error with clear message\n799\t    - Includes test cases for CORS header presence and values\n800\t    - Function is exported from test utils module\n801\t    \"\"\"\n802\t    # Extract URL for error messages\n803\t    url: Optional[str] = None\n804\t    if hasattr(response, 'url'):\n805\t        url = getattr(response, 'url', None)\n806\t\n807\t    # Extract CORS headers from response\n808\t    cors_headers = _extract_cors_headers(response)\n809\t\n810\t    # Check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin is present when required\n811\t    if require_allow_origin:\n812\t        allow_origin = cors_headers.get('access-control-allow-origin')\n813\t        if not allow_origin:\n814\t            if throw_on_error:\n815\t                raise CORSValidationError(\n816\t                    message=\"Missing required CORS header: Access-Control-Allow-Origin\",\n817\t                    actual_headers=cors_headers,\n818\t                    url=url\n819\t                )\n820\t            return False\n821\t\n822\t    # If no expected_origin is specified, just check for presence\n823\t    if expected_origin is None:\n824\t        # We already checked for presence above if require_allow_origin is True\n825\t        # If require_allow_origin is False and no expected_origin, we pass\n826\t        return True\n827\t\n828\t    # Validate the origin value\n829\t    allow_origin = cors_headers.get('access-control-allow-origin', '')\n830\t\n831\t    # Check for wildcard\n832\t    if allow_origin == '*':\n833\t        if allow_wildcard:\n834\t            return True\n835\t        else:\n836\t            if throw_on_error:\n837\t                raise CORSValidationError(\n838\t                    message=\"Wildcard origin (*) is not allowed when specific origin is expected\",\n839\t                    actual_headers=cors_headers,\n840\t                    expected_origin=expected_origin,\n841\t                    url=url\n842\t                )\n843\t            return False\n844\t\n845\t    # Check for exact origin match\n846\t    if allow_origin == expected_origin:\n847\t        return True\n848\t\n849\t    # Origin doesn't match\n850\t    if throw_on_error:\n851\t        raise CORSValidationError(\n852\t            message=f\"Origin mismatch: expected '{expected_origin}' but got '{allow_origin}'\",\n853\t            actual_headers=cors_headers,\n854\t            expected_origin=expected_origin,\n855\t            url=url\n856\t        )\n857\t    return False\n858\t\n859\t\n860\tdef validate_cors_allow_origin(\n861\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n862\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n863\t) -> bool:\n864\t    \"\"\"\n865\t    Validate response has Access-Control-Allow-Origin header.\n866\t\n867\t    This is a convenience function that only checks for presence of the header,\n868\t    regardless of its value.\n869\t\n870\t    Args:\n871\t        response: HTTP response object\n872\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws on validation failure\n873\t\n874\t    Returns:\n875\t        bool: True if Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is present\n876\t    \"\"\"\n877\t    return validate_cors_headers(\n878\t        response,\n879\t        require_allow_origin=True,\n880\t        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n881\t    )\n882\t\n883\t\n884\tdef validate_cors_wildcard(\n885\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n886\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n887\t) -> bool:\n888\t    \"\"\"\n889\t    Validate response has wildcard CORS origin.\n890\t\n891\t    Checks that Access-Control-Allow-Origin is set to '*'.\n892\t\n893\t    Args:\n894\t        response: HTTP response object\n895\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws on validation failure\n896\t\n897\t    Returns:\n898\t        bool: True if CORS allows any origin\n899\t    \"\"\"\n900\t    return validate_cors_headers(\n901\t        response,\n902\t        expected_origin='*',\n903\t        allow_wildcard=True,\n904\t        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n905\t    )\n906\t\n907\t\n908\tdef validate_cors_specific_origin(\n909\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n910\t    origin: str,\n911\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n912\t) -> bool:\n913\t    \"\"\"\n914\t    Validate response has specific CORS origin.\n915\t\n916\t    Checks that Access-Control-Allow-Origin matches the given origin exactly.\n917\t    Rejects wildcard unless the wildcard is the expected origin.\n918\t\n919\t    Args:\n920\t        response: HTTP response object\n921\t        origin: The expected origin value (e.g., 'https://example.com')\n922\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws on validation failure\n923\t\n924\t    Returns:\n925\t        bool: True if CORS allows the specific origin\n926\t    \"\"\"\n927\t    return validate_cors_headers(\n928\t        response,\n929\t        expected_origin=origin,\n930\t        allow_wildcard=(origin == '*'),  # Only allow wildcard if it's the expected value\n931\t        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n932\t    )\n933\t\n934\t\n935\tdef validate_cors_credentials(\n936\t    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n937\t    throw_on_error: bool = True\n938\t) -> bool:\n939\t    \"\"\"\n940\t    Validate response has Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header.\n941\t\n942\t    Checks for presence of Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header.\n943\t    Useful for APIs that support cookies or authentication.\n944\t\n945\t    Args:\n946\t        response: HTTP response object\n947\t        throw_on_error: If True, throws on validation failure\n948\t\n949\t    Returns:\n950\t        bool: True if Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header is present\n951\t    \"\"\"\n952\t    cors_headers = _extract_cors_headers(response)\n953\t\n954\t    allow_creds = cors_headers.get('access-control-allow-credentials')\n955\t    if not allow_creds:\n956\t        if throw_on_error:\n957\t            url = getattr(response, 'url', None) if hasattr(response, 'url') else None\n958\t            raise CORSValidationError(\n959\t                message=\"Missing required CORS header: Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\",\n960\t                actual_headers=cors_headers,\n961\t                url=url\n962\t            )\n963\t        return False\n964\t\n965\t    return True\n966\t\n967\t\n968\t# The actual status code received\n    - The expected status code(s)\n    - The response body (truncated if too long)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self,\n                 actual: int,\n                 expected: Union[int, List[int]],\n                 response_body: Optional[str] = None,\n                 url: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize a status validation error.\n\n        Args:\n            actual: The actual HTTP status code received\n            expected: The expected status code or list of allowed codes\n            response_body: Optional response body for debugging\n            url: Optional URL that was requested\n        \"\"\"\n        self.actual = actual\n        self.expected = expected\n        self.response_body = response_body\n        self.url = url\n\n        # Build detailed error message\n        msg_parts = []\n\n        if url:\n            msg_parts.append(f\"URL: {url}\")\n\n        msg_parts.append(f\"Expected HTTP status code: {self._format_expected()}\")\n        msg_parts.append(f\"Actual status code: {actual}\")\n\n        if response_body:\n            # Truncate response body if too long\n            body_preview = response_body[:200]\n            if len(response_body) > 200:\n                body_preview += \"... throw_on_error: bool = True\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate HTTP response status code against expected value(s).\n\n    This helper function validates that an HTTP response has the expected status code.\n    It supports both single status codes and lists of allowed codes, making it\n    flexible for different testing scenarios (e.g., accepting both 200 and 204).\n\n    Args:\n        response: HTTP response object (requests.Response) or tuple of (status_code, body)\n        expected_status: Expected HTTP status code (int) or list of allowed codes (List[int])\n       )\n\n    # Check if actual status matches any expected code\n    is_valid = actual_status in expected_codes\n\n    if not is_valid and throw_on_error:\n        # Determine which single status to Content-Type: {actual}\")\n\n        message = \"\\n  \".join(msg_parts)\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n    def _format_expected(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format expected content-types for       return f\"{self.expected[0]} or {self.expected[1]}\"\n            else:\n                return f\"one of {self.expected}\"\n        else:\n            return str(self.expected)\n\n\ndef _normalize_content_type(content_type: Optional[str]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Normalize a content-type string for comparison.\n\n    This function:\n    - Converts to lowercase\n    - Trims whitespace\n    - Extracts the MIME type without parameters (e.g., 'application/json; charset=utf-8' -> 'application/json')\n\n    Args:\n        content_type: The content-type string to normalize (can be None)\n\n    Returns:\n        str: Normalized content-type, or empty string if input is None\n    \"\"\"\n    if not content_type:\n        return \"\"\n\n    # Convert to lowercase and trim whitespace\n    content_type = content_type.lower().strip()\n\n    # Extract only the MIME type (before semicolon if present)\n    # This handles cases like 'application/json; charset=utf-8' -> 'application/json'\n    if ';' in content_type:\n        content_type = content_type.split(';')[0].strip()\n\n    return content_type\n\n\ndef validate_content_type(\n    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n    expected_type: Union[str, List[str]],\n    throw_on_error: bool = True\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate HTTP response content-type header against expected value(s).\n\n    This helper function validates that an HTTP response has the expected content-type.\n    It supports both single content-types and lists of allowed types, making it\n    flexible for different testing scenarios. The function uses pattern matching,\n    so 'application/json' will match 'application/json; charset=utf-8'.\n\n    Args:\n        response: HTTP response object (requests.Response) or tuple of (status_code, headers, body)\n        expected_type: Expected content-type (str) or list of allowed types (List[str])\n        throw_on_error: If True, throws ContentTypeValidationError on validation failure.\n                       If False, returns False instead.\n\n    Returns:\n        bool: True if content-type matches expected value(s), False otherwise\n\n    Raises:\n        ContentTypeValidationError: If validation fails and throw_on_error is True\n        TypeError: If response or expected_type has invalid type\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> # Single content-type validation\n        >>> response = requests.get('http://example.com/api')\n        >>> validate_content_type(response, 'application/json')\n\n        >>> # Multiple allowed content-types\n        >>> validate_content_type(response, ['application/json', 'application/xml'])\n\n        >>> # Pattern matching (matches both 'application/json' and 'application/json; charset=utf-8')\n        >>> validate_content_type(response, 'application/json')\n\n        >>> # Using tuple response format\n        >>> status, headers, body = curl_request(...)\n        >>> validate_content_type((status, headers, body), 'text/html')\n\n        >>> # Non-throwing validation\n        >>> is_valid = validate_content_type(response, 'application/json', throw_on_error=False)\n        >>> if is_valid:\n        ...     print(\"Content-type is valid!\")\n\n    Acceptance Criteria:\n    - Function accepts a response object and expected content-type(s)\n    - Supports pattern matching (e.g., 'application/json' matches 'application/json; charset=utf-8')\n    - Returns boolean or throws assertion error with clear message\n    - Includes test cases demonstrating various content-type scenarios\n    - Function is exported from test utils module\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract content-type and URL from response\n    actual_content_type: Optional[str] = None\n    url: Optional[str] = None\n\n    # Check for response-like objects (duck typing)\n    # First check if it's a tuple (special case)\n    if isinstance(response, tuple) and len(response) >= 2:\n        # Tuple format: (status_code, headers, body) or (status_code, headers)\n        if len(response) >= 2:\n            headers = response[1]\n            if isinstance(headers, dict):\n                actual_content_type = headers.get('Content-Type', headers.get('content-type'))\n            elif isinstance(headers, str):\n                # Headers might be a string in some test scenarios\n                actual_content_type = headers\n    # Then check if it has response-like attributes (headers, etc.)\n    elif hasattr(response, 'headers'):\n        actual_content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type',\n                                                     response.headers.get('content-type', ''))\n        url = getattr(response, 'url', None)\n    else:\n        raise TypeError(\n            f\"Response must be response-like (with headers attribute) or tuple (status, headers, body), \"\n            f\"got {type(response).__name__}\"\n        )\n\n    # Normalize the actual content-type for comparison\n    normalized_actual = _normalize_content_type(actual_content_type)\n\n    # Normalize expected_type to a list for uniform checking\n    expected_types: List[str]\n    if isinstance(expected_type, str):\n        expected_types = [expected_type]\n    elif isinstance(expected_type, list):\n        expected_types = expected_type\n    else:\n        raise TypeError(\n            f\"expected_type must be str or List[str], \"\n            f\"got {type(expected_type).__name__}\"\n        )\n\n    # Validate all expected types are strings and normalize them\n    normalized_expected = []\n    for ct in expected_types:\n        if not isinstance(ct, str):\n            raise TypeError(\n                f\"All content-types must be strings, got {type(ct).__name__}\"\n            )\n        normalized_expected.append(_normalize_content_type(ct))\n\n    # Check if actual content-type matches any expected type\n    is_valid = normalized_actual in normalized_expected\n\n    if not is_valid and throw_on_error:\n        # Use the original expected_type (not normalized) for error message\n        error_expected = expected_type\n        raise ContentTypeValidationError(\n            actual=actual_content_type or \"(missing)\",\n            expected=error_expected,\n            url=url\n        )\n\n    return is_valid\n\n\ndef validate_json_content_type(\n    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n    throw_on_error: bool = True\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate response has JSON content-type.\n\n    Accepts any JSON content-type variant (application/json, text/json, etc.).\n\n    Args:\n        response: HTTP response object\n        throw_on_error: If True, throws on validation failure\n\n    Returns:\n        bool: True if content-type indicates JSON\n    \"\"\"\n    json_types = [\n        'application/json',\n        'text/json',\n        'application/vnd.api+json',\n        'application/problem+json'\n    ]\n    return validate_content_type(\n        response,\n        expected_type=json_types,\n        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n    )\n\n\ndef validate_xml_content_type(\n    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n    throw_on_error: bool = True\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate response has XML content-type.\n\n    Accepts any XML  expected_type='text/plain',\n        throw_on_error=throw_on_error\n    )\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# CORS HEADER VALIDATION\n# =============================================================================\n\nclass CORSValidationError(AssertionError):\n    \"\"\"\n    Custom assertion error for CORS header validation failures.\n\n    Provides clear, formatted error messages that show:\n    - The actual CORS headers received\n    - The expected CORS headers or origin values\n    - The response URL (if available)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self,\n                 message: str,\n                 actual_headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,\n                 expected_origin: Optional[str] = None,\n                 url: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize a CORS validation error.\n\n        Args:\n            message: The main error message\n            actual_headers: The actual CORS headers received\n            expected_origin: The expected origin value\n            url: Optional URL that was requested\n        \"\"\"\n        self.actual_headers = actual_headers or {}\n        self.expected_origin = expected_origin\n        self.url = url\n\n        # Build detailed error message\n        msg_parts = []\n\n        if url:\n            msg_parts.append(f\"URL: {url}\")\n\n        msg_parts.append(message)\n\n        if expected_origin:\n            msg_parts.append(f\"Expected origin: {expected_origin}\")\n\n        if actual_headers:\n            msg_parts.append(\"Actual CORS headers:\")\n            for header, value in actual_headers.items():\n                msg_parts.append(f\"  {header}: {value}\")\n\n        super().__init__(\"\\n  \".join(msg_parts))\n\n\ndef _extract_cors_headers(response: Union[requests.Response, tuple]) -> Dict[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract CORS headers from a response object.\n\n    Args:\n        response: HTTP response object (requests.Response) or tuple of (status_code, headers, body)\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict[str, str]: Dictionary of CORS headers found in the response\n    \"\"\"\n    cors_headers = {}\n\n    # Common CORS header names (both lowercase and titlecase)\n    cors_header_names = [\n        'access-control-allow-origin',\n        'Access-Control-Allow-Origin',\n        'access-control-allow-methods',\n        'Access-Control-Allow-Methods',\n        'access-control-allow-headers',\n        'Access-Control-Allow-Headers',\n        'access-control-allow-credentials',\n        'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials',\n        'access-control-expose-headers',\n        'Access-Control-Expose-Headers',\n        'access-control-max-age',\n        'Access-Control-Max-Age',\n    ]\n\n    # Check for response-like objects (duck typing)\n    if isinstance(response, tuple) and len(response) >= 2:\n        # Tuple format: (status_code, headers, body) or (status_code, headers)\n        headers = response[1]\n        if isinstance(headers, dict):\n            for header_name in cors_header_names:\n                if header_name in headers:\n                    # Normalize to lowercase for consistency\n                    normalized_name = header_name.lower()\n                    cors_headers[normalized_name] = headers[header_name]\n    elif hasattr(response, 'headers'):\n        # requests.Response-like object\n        for header_name in cors_header_names:\n            if header_name in response.headers:\n                normalized_name = header_name.lower()\n                cors_headers[normalized_name] = response.headers[header_name]\n\n    return cors_headers\n\n\ndef validate_cors_headers(\n    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n    expected_origin: Optional[str] = None,\n    allow_wildcard: bool = True,\n    require_allow_origin: bool = True,\n    throw_on_error: bool = True\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate CORS headers in an HTTP response.\n\n    This helper function validates that an HTTP response has proper CORS headers.\n    It supports checking for the presence of Access-Control-Allow-Origin, validating\n    specific origin values, and distinguishing between wildcard (*) and specific origins.\n\n    Args:\n        response: HTTP response object (requests.Response) or tuple of (status_code, headers, body)\n        expected_origin: Optional specific origin value to validate (e.g., 'https://example.com')\n                        If None, only checks for presence of CORS headers\n        allow_wildcard: If True, accepts wildcard (*) as a valid origin\n        require_allow_origin: If True, requires Access-Control-Allow-Origin header to be present\n        throw_on_error: If True, throws CORSValidationError on validation failure.\n                       If False, returns False instead.\n\n    Returns:\n        bool: True if CORS headers are valid, False otherwise\n\n    Raises:\n        CORSValidationError: If validation fails and throw_on_error is True\n        TypeError: If response has invalid type\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> # Check for any CORS headers present\n        >>> response = requests.get('http://example.com/api')\n        >>> validate_cors_headers(response)\n\n        >>> # Validate specific origin\n        >>> validate_cors_headers(response, expected_origin='https://myapp.com')\n\n        >>> # Reject wildcard, require specific origin\n        >>> validate_cors_headers(response, expected_origin='https://myapp.com', allow_wildcard=False)\n\n        >>> # Using tuple response format\n        >>> status, headers, body = curl_request(...)\n        >>> validate_cors_headers((status, headers, body), expected_origin='https://example.com')\n\n        >>> # Non-throwing validation\n        >>> is_valid = validate_cors_headers(response, expected_origin='https://example.com', throw_on_error=False)\n        >>> if is_valid:\n        ...     print(\"CORS headers are valid!\")\n\n    Acceptance Criteria:\n    - Function accepts a response object and validates required CORS headers\n    - Checks for Access-Control-Allow-Origin header\n    - Optionally validates specific origin values\n    - Supports checking for wildcard vs specific origins\n    - Returns boolean or throws assertion error with clear message\n    - Includes test cases for CORS header presence and values\n    - Function is exported from test utils module\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract URL for error messages\n    url: Optional[str] = None\n    if hasattr(response, 'url'):\n        url = getattr(response, 'url', None)\n\n    # Extract CORS headers from response\n    cors_headers = _extract_cors_headers(response)\n\n    # Check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin is present when required\n    if require_allow_origin:\n        allow_origin = cors_headers.get('access-control-allow-origin')\n        if not allow_origin:\n            if throw_on_error:\n                raise CORSValidationError(\n                    message=\"Missing required CORS header: Access-Control-Allow-Origin\",\n                    actual_headers=cors_headers,\n                    url=url\n                )\n            return False\n\n    # If no expected_origin is specified, just check for presence\n    if expected_origin is None:\n        # We already checked for presence above if require_allow_origin is True\n        # If require_allow_origin is False and no expected_origin, we pass\n        return True\n\n    # Validate the origin value\n    allow_origin = cors_headers.get('access-control-allow-origin', '')\n\n    # Check for wildcard\n    if allow_origin == '*':\n        if allow_wildcard:\n            return True\n        else:\n            if throw_on_error:\n                raise CORSValidationError(\n                    message=\"Wildcard origin (*) is not allowed when specific origin is expected\",\n                    actual_headers=cors_headers,\n                    expected_origin=expected_origin,\n                    url=url\n                )\n            return False\n\n    # Check for exact origin match\n    if allow_origin == expected_origin:\n        return True\n\n    # Origin doesn't match\n    if throw_on_error:\n        raise CORSValidationError(\n            message=f\"Origin mismatch: expected '{expected_origin}' but got '{allow_origin}'\",\n            actual_headers=cors_headers,\n            expected_origin=expected_origin,\n            url=url\n        )\n    return False\n\n\ndef validate_cors_allow_origin(\n    response: Union[requests.Response, tuple],\n    throw_on_error: bool = True\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate response has Access-Control-Allow-Origin header.\n\n    This is a convenience function that only          )\n        return False\n\n    return True\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# HTTP REQUEST HELPER\n# =============================================================================\n\nclass HTTPRequestError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Custom exception for HTTP request failures.\n\n    Provides clear error messages that include:\n    - The HTTP method and URL\n    - The error type (connection, timeout, etc.)\n    - The underlying error message\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, method: str, url: str, error_type: str, message: str):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize an HTTP request error.\n\n        Args:\n            method: The HTTP method used (GET, POST, etc.)\n            url: The URL that was requested\n            error_type: Type of error (connection, timeout, etc.)\n            message: Detailed error message\n        \"\"\"\n        self.method = method\n        self.url = url\n        self.error_type = error_type\n\n        super().__init__(\n            f\"HTTP {method} request to {url} failed: {error_type}\\n  {message}\"\n        )\n\n\ndef make_http_request(\n    base_url: str,\n    path: str = \"\",\n    method: str = \"GET\",\n    headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,\n    body: Optional[Union[str, Dict[str, Any], bytes]] = None,\n    timeout: int = 30,\n    raise_on_error: bool = False\n) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Make an HTTP request to a test server.\n\n    This helper function provides a simple interface for making HTTP requests\n    to a test server. It supports all common HTTP methods, custom headers,\n    request bodies, and graceful error handling.\n\n    Args:\n        base_url: The base URL of the test server (e.g., 'http://localhost:8080')\n        path: Optional path to append to the base URL (e.g., '/api/users')\n        method: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS). Defaults to 'GET'.\n        headers: Optional dictionary of HTTP headers to include in the request\n        body: Optional request body (string, dict for JSON, or bytes)\n        timeout: Request timeout in seconds (default: 30)\n        raise_on_error: If True, raises HTTPRequestError on connection errors.\n                       If False, returns error info in the response dict.\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict[str, Any]: A dictionary containing:\n            - 'success': bool - True if request succeeded, False on error\n            - 'status_code': int - HTTP status code (0 on connection error)\n            - 'headers': Dict[str, str] - Response headers (empty on error)\n            - 'body': str - Response body as string (error message on connection error)\n            - 'url': str - The full URL that was requested\n            - 'method': str - The HTTP method used\n            - 'error': Optional[str] - Error type if connection failed (e.g., 'connection', 'timeout')\n\n    Raises:\n        HTTPRequestError: If raise_on_error is True and a connection error occurs\n        ValueError: If requests library is not available or invalid parameters\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> # Simple GET request\n        >>> response = make_http_request('http://localhost:8080', '/api/health')\n        >>> if response['success']:\n        ...     print(f\"Status: {response['status_code']}\")\n        ...     print(f\"Body: {response['body']}\")\n\n        >>> # POST request with JSON body\n        >>> import json\n        >>> data = {'name': 'test', 'value': 123}\n        >>> response = make_http_request(\n        ...     'http://localhost:8080',\n        ...     '/api/users',\n        ...     method='POST',\n        ...     headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'},\n        ...     body=json.dumps(data)\n        ... )\n\n        >>> # PUT request with custom headers\n        >>> response = make_http_request(\n        ...     'http://localhost:8080',\n        ...     '/api/users/123',\n        ...     method='PUT',\n        ...     headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer token123'},\n        ...     body='{\"name\": \"updated\"}'\n        ... )\n\n        >>> # DELETE request\n        >>> response = make_http_request(\n        ...     'http://localhost:8080',\n        ...     '/api/users/123',\n        ...     method='DELETE'\n        ... )\n\n        >>> # With automatic JSON serialization (dict body)\n        >>> response = make_http_request(\n        ...     'http://localhost:8080',\n        ...     '/api/data',\n        ...     method='POST',\n        ...     body={'key': 'value'}  # Automatically serialized to JSON\n        ... )\n\n        >>> # With error handling\n        >>> try:\n        ...     response = make_http_request(\n        ...         'http://localhost:8080',\n        ...         '/api/test',\n        ...         raise_on_error=True\n        ...     )\n        ... except HTTPRequestError as e:\n        ...     print(f\"Request failed: {e}\")\n\n    Acceptance Criteria:\n    - Function makes HTTP requests to a test server ✓\n    - Supports configurable HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) ✓\n    - Allows setting request headers and body ✓\n    - Returns the full response including status code, headers, and body ✓\n    - Handles connection errors gracefully ✓\n    - Includes basic usage examples in comments ✓\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if requests library is available\n    if not REQUESTS_AVAILABLE:\n        raise ValueError(\n            \"The 'requests' library is required for make_http_request but is not installed. \"\n            \"Install it with: pip install requests\"\n        )\n\n    # Validate and normalize HTTP method\n    method_upper = method.upper()\n    valid_methods = {'GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'PATCH', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS'}\n    if method_upper not in valid_methods:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Invalid HTTP method '{method}'. Must be one of: {', '.join(sorted(valid_methods))}\"\n        )\n\n    # Construct full URL\n    url = base_url.rstrip('/')\n    if path:\n        url += '/' + path.lstrip('/')\n\n    # Prepare headers\n    request_headers = (headers or {}).copy()\n\n    # Prepare body\n    request_body = body\n    if isinstance(body, dict):\n        # Automatically serialize dict to JSON\n        import json\n        request_body = json.dumps(body)\n        # Set Content-Type if not already set\n        if 'Content-Type' not in request_headers and 'content-type' not in request_headers:\n            request_headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'\n\n    try:\n        # Make the HTTP request using the requests library\n        response = requests.request(\n            method=method_upper,\n            url=url,\n            headers=request_headers,\n            data=request_body,\n            timeout=timeout\n        )\n\n        return {\n            'success': True,\n            'status_code': response.status_code,\n            'headers': dict(response.headers),\n            'body': response.text,\n            'url': url,\n            'method': method_upper,\n            'error': None\n        }\n\n    except requests.exceptions.ConnectionError as e:\n        error_msg = f\"Connection error: {str(e)}\"\n        if raise_on_error:\n            raise HTTPRequestError(method_upper, url, 'connection', str(e)) from e\n\n        return {\n            'success': False,\n            'status_code': 0,\n            'headers': {},\n            'body': error_msg,\n            'url': url,\n            'method': method_upper,\n            'error': 'connection'\n        }\n\n    except requests.exceptions.Timeout as e:\n        error_msg = f\"Request timed out after {timeout}s: {str(e)}\"\n        if raise_on_error:\n            raise HTTPRequestError(method_upper, url, 'timeout', str(e)) from e\n\n        return {\n            'success': False,\n            'status_code': 0,\n            'headers': {},\n            'body': error_msg,\n            'url': url,\n            'method': method_upper,\n            'error': 'timeout'\n        }\n\n    except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:\n        error_msg = f\"HTTP error: {str(e)}\"\n        if raise_on_error:\n            raise HTTPRequestError(method_upper, url, 'http_error', str(e)) from e\n\n        return {\n            'success': False,\n            'status_code': 0,\n            'headers': {},\n            'body': error_msg,\n            'url': url,\n            'method': method_upper,\n            'error': 'http_error'\n        }\n\n    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:\n        error_msg = f\"Request failed: {str(e)}\"\n        if raise_on_error:\n            raise HTTPRequestError(method_upper, url, 'request_error', str(e)) from e\n\n        return {\n            'success': False,\n            'status_code': 0,\n            'headers': {},\n            'body': error_msg,\n            'url': url,\n            'method': method_upper,\n            'error': 'request_error'\n        }\n","numLines":1213,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1213}}}

   validate_http_status(response, [200, 201])\n\n        error_message = str(context.exception)\n        self.assertIn(\"200 or 201\", error_message)\n\n    def test_single_element_list(self):\n validate_http_status(response, allowed_codes, throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertTrue(result, \"Should handle large lists of allowed codes\")\n\n\nclass TestMultipleResponseValidation(unittest.TestCase):\n    \"\"\"Test validation of multiple responses at once.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_validate_multiple_responses_all_pass(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validating multiple responses where all pass.\"\"\"\n        responses = [\n            MockResponse(status_code=200),\n            MockResponse(status_code=200),\n            MockResponse(status_code=200)\n        ]\n\n        results = validate_http_status_codes(*responses, expected_status=200, throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertEqual(len(results), 3, \"Should return result for each response\")\n        self.assertTrue(all(results), \"All responses should pass validation\")\n\n    def test_validate_multiple_responses_some_fail(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validating multiple responses where some fail.\"\"\"\n        responses = [\n            MockResponse(status_code=200),\n            MockResponse(status_code=404),\n            MockResponse(status_code=200)\n        ]\n\n        results = validate_http_status_codes(*responses, expected_status=200, throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertEqual(results, [True, False, True], \"Should track individual results\")\n\n    def test_validate_multiple_throws_on_first_failure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation throws on first failure when throw_on_error=True.\"\"\"\n        responses = [\n            MockResponse(status_code=200),\n            MockResponse(status_code=404),\n            MockResponse(status_code=500)  # Should never reach this\n        ]\n\n        with self.assertRaises(StatusValidationError):\n            validate_http_status_codes(*responses, expected_status=200, throw_on_error=True)\n\n    def test_validate_multiple_no_throw_returns_all_results(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation returns all results when not throwing.\"\"\"\n        responses = [\n            MockResponse(status_code=200, text=\"OK\"),\n            MockResponse(status_code=404, text=\"Not Found\"),\n            MockResponse(status_code=500, text=\"Server Error\")\n        ]\n\n        results = validate_http_status_codes(*responses, expected_status=200, throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertEqual(len(results), 3)\n        self.assertEqual(results, [True, False, False])\n\n\nclass integers\", str(context.exception))\n\n    def test_empty_allowed_list(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation with empty list of allowed codes.\"\"\"\n        response = validate_http_status(response, [401, 403])\n\n    def test_created_response(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validating resource creation.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=201, text=\"Created\", url=\"http://api.example.com/users\")\n\n        # Accept both 201 and 202 (some APIs process async)\n        validate_http_status(response, [201, 202])\n\n    def that error messages provide helpful debugging information.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_error_message_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test error messages have proper structure.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            text=\"Database connection failed\",\n            url=\"http://api.example.com/users\"\n        )\n\n        with self.assertRaises(StatusValidationError) as context:\n            validate_http_status(response, 200)\n\n        error_msg = str(context.exception)\n\n        # Should contain key information\n        self.assertIn(\"URL:\", error_msg)\n        self.assertIn(\"Expected HTTP status code:\", error_msg)\n        self.assertIn(\"Actual status code:\", error_msg)\n        self.assertIn(\"Response body:\", error_msg)\n\n    def test_error_without_body_still_useful(self):\n        \"\"\"Test error message is useful even without response body.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=404, text=\"\", url=\"http://example.com\")\n\n        with self.assertRaises(StatusValidationError) as context:\n            validate_http_status(response, 200)\n\n        error_msg = str(context.exception)\n        self.assertIn(\"404\", error_msg)\n        self.assertIn(\"200\", error_msg)\n\n    def test_error_without_url_still_useful(self):\n        \"\"\"Test error message is useful even without URL.\"\"\"\n        response = (500, \"Server Error\")\n\n        with self.assertRaises(StatusValidationError) as context:\n            validate_http_status(response, 200)\n\n        error_msg = str(context.exception)\n        self.assertIn(\"500\", error_msg)\n        self.assertIn(\"200\", error_msg)\n        self.assertIn(\"Server Error\", error_msg)\n\n\n# TestContentTypeValidationBasic(unittest.TestCase):\n    \"\"\"Test basic content-type validation functionality.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_valid_content_type_exact_match(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation passes with exact content-type match.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=200, content_type='application/json')\n\n        result = validate_content_type(response, 'application/json', throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertTrue(result, \"Should accept exact content-type match\")\n\n    def test_valid_content_type_pattern_match(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation passes with pattern matching (ignoring charset).\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=200,\n                                content_type='application/json; charset=utf-8')\n\n        result = validate_content_type(response, 'application/json', throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertTrue(result, \"Should match content-type pattern ignoring parameters\")\n\n    def test_valid_content_type_with_charset(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation handles charset parameter correctly.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=200,\n                                content_type='text/html; charset=utf-8')\n\n        result = validate_content_type(response, 'text/html', throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertTrue(result, \"Should handle charset parameter\")\n\n    def test_valid_content_type_case_insensitive(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation is case-insensitive.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=200,\n                                content_type='Application/JSON; Charset=UTF-8')\n\n        result = validate_content_type(response, 'application/json', throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertTrue(result, \"Should handle case-insensitive matching\")\n\n    def test_invalid_content_type_returns_false(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation failure returns False when not throwing.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=200, content_type='text/html')\n\n        result = validate_content_type(response, 'application/json', throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertFalse(result, \"Should reject non-matching content-type\")\n\n    def test_invalid_content_type_throws_error(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation failure throws ContentTypeValidationError by default.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=200, content_type='text/html')\n\n        with self.assertRaises(ContentTypeValidationError) as context:\n            validate_content_type(response, 'application/json')\n\n        error = context.exception\n        self.assertIn('text/html', str(error))\n        self.assertIn('application/json', str(error))\n\n    def test_missing_content_type(self):\n        \"\"\"Test validation handles missing Content-Type header.\"\"\"\n        response = MockResponse(status_code=200)  # No content-type set\n\n        result = validate_content_type(response, 'application/json', throw_on_error=False)\n\n        self.assertFalse(result, \"Should reject missing content-type\")\n\n\nclass HTTP Request Helper Implementation (bf-3aj0bn)\n\n## Overview\nImplemented a helper function to make HTTP requests to the test server in the ARMOR test suite.\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Files Modified\n1. `/home/coding/ARMOR/tests/test_helpers.py` - Added HTTP request helper function\n2. `/home/coding/ARMOR/tests/__init__.py` - Exported new helper functions\n\n### New Components\n\n#### HTTPRequestError Exception\nCustom exception for HTTP request failures with clear error messages including:\n- HTTP method and URL\n- Error type (connection, timeout, etc.)\n- Underlying error message\n\n#### make_http_request Function\nComplete HTTP request helper with the following features:\n\n**Supported HTTP Methods:**\n- GET\n- POST\n- PUT\n- DELETE\n- PATCH\n- HEAD\n- OPTIONS\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `base_url` (str): Base URL of the test server\n- `path` (str, optional): Path to append to base URL\n- `method` (str, optional): HTTP method (default: \"GET\")\n- `headers` (Dict[str, str], optional): Request headers\n- `body` (Optional[Union[str, Dict, bytes]]): Request body\n- `timeout` (int, optional): Request timeout in seconds (default: 30)\n- `raise_on_error` (bool, optional): Raise exception on error (default: False)\n\n**Response Structure:**\n```python\n{\n    'success': bool,           # True if request succeeded\n    'status_code': int,        # HTTP status code (0 on error)\n    'headers': Dict[str, str], # Response headers\n    'body': str,               # Response body\n    'url': str,                # Full URL requested\n    'method': str,             # HTTP method used\n    'error': Optional[str]     # Error type if failed\n}\n```\n\n**Error Handling:**\n- Connection errors → Returns error info or raises HTTPRequestError\n- Timeout errors → Returns error info or raises HTTPRequestError\n- HTTP errors → Returns error info or raises HTTPRequestError\n- Invalid HTTP method → Raises ValueError\n- Missing requests library → Raises ValueError with installation instructions\n\n**Automatic JSON Serialization:**\n- Dict bodies are automatically serialized to JSON\n- Content-Type header set automatically if not provided\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Met\n✅ Function makes HTTP requests to a test server\n✅ Supports configurable HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS)\n✅ Allows setting request headers and body\n✅ Returns full response including status code, headers, and body\n✅ Handles connection errors gracefully\n✅ Includes basic usage examples in docstring comments\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n```python\nfrom tests import make_http_request, HTTPRequestError\n\n# Simple GET request\nresponse = make_http_request('http://localhost:8080', '/api/health')\nif response['success']:\n    print(f\"Status: {response['status_code']}\")\n\n# POST request with JSON body\nresponse = make_http_request(\n    'http://localhost:8080',\n    '/api/users',\n    method='POST',\n    headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'},\n    body={'name': 'test', 'value': 123}  # Auto-serialized to JSON\n)\n\n# PUT request with custom headers\nresponse = make_http_request(\n    'http://localhost:8080',\n    '/api/users/123',\n    method='PUT',\n    headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer token123'},\n    body='{\"name\": \"updated\"}'\n)\n\n# DELETE request\nresponse = make_http_request(\n    'http://localhost:8080',\n    '/api/users/123',\n    method='DELETE'\n)\n\n# With exception handling\ntry:\n    response = make_http_request(\n        'http://localhost:8080',\n        '/api/test',\n        raise_on_error=True\n    )\nexcept HTTPRequestError as e:\n    print(f\"Request failed: {e}\")\n```\n\n## Design Decisions\n\n1. **Followed existing patterns**: Used the same optional `requests` import pattern as existing code in `test_helpers.py`\n2. **Consistent return format**: Returns a dictionary similar to other test helpers for consistency\n3. **Graceful error handling**: Returns error info by default but can raise exceptions if needed\n4. **Automatic JSON handling**: Dict bodies are automatically serialized to JSON for convenience\n5. **Comprehensive examples**: Docstring includes multiple usage examples for common scenarios\n\n## Notes\n- The function requires the `requests` library to be installed\n- If `requests` is not available, a clear error message is provided\n- The implementation follows the existing code style and patterns in the test suite\n- All functions are properly exported from `tests/__init__.py`\n","numLines":132,"startLine":1,"totalLines":132}}}
